prerequisites: A Sage HR account with admin privileges, since admin rights are required to enable API access and removing those rights invalidates the key.
Your company subdomain, the company-specific prefix of your Sage HR sign-in address (for example, the acme in acme.sage.hr), used to identify your account's API host.
An API key generated from the Sage HR Settings > Integrations > API screen.

authMethods.name: API key (X-Auth-Token header)
authMethods.summary: Sage HR authenticates with a single API key. The key is sent in the X-Auth-Token HTTP header on every request and maps to the connector's api_key secret field. The key is tied to the admin user who generated it; if that user loses admin access the key stops working.

setupSteps.title: Find your Sage HR subdomain
setupSteps.body: Your subdomain is the company-specific prefix of your Sage HR web address. If you sign in at acme.sage.hr, the subdomain is acme. You will enter this in the connector's subdomain config field, which selects the company-specific API host that requests are sent to.

setupSteps.title: Generate your API key in Sage HR
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Sage HR as an admin, click your name in the top-right corner, and select Settings. In the left-hand menu open Integrations, then choose API and enable API access. Generate or copy the API key shown there. The key is displayed once at generation time, so copy and store it securely; re-enabling API access invalidates the previous key.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the subdomain from step 1 into the subdomain field and the API key from step 2 into the api_key field. The connector sends the key in the X-Auth-Token header on each request to your company's Sage HR API host.

setupSteps.title: Validate the connection
setupSteps.body: Run the connection check. A 401 or 403 response means the X-Auth-Token value is wrong or API access is not enabled for that account; confirm the key belongs to a current admin user and that the subdomain is correct.
